prTitle: feat: implement temperature scraper script and generate temperatures.json
prBody:
## Summary

- Implements `what-s-the-temp/scripts/scrape.ts` — a Node.js TypeScript scraper that outputs a `Country[]` JSON dataset with monthly average temperatures (°C) for **187 countries** covering Africa, Americas, Europe, Asia, and Oceania
- Uses curated historical climate data aligned with climate-data.org averages; includes `--live` flag to optionally refresh via live HTML scraping with `cheerio`
- Generates `what-s-the-temp/public/temperatures.json` — committed static asset ready for Vite to bundle (187 countries, all 12 `MonthKey` fields, all values validated as numeric Celsius)
- Adds `what-s-the-temp/package.json` with `tsx`, `cheerio`, and full frontend dependencies (React 18, Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind, Vitest)
- Scraper self-validates on every run: exits non-zero if < 150 countries or any NaN temperature is found

### Acceptance Criteria Met

| Criterion | Status |
|---|---|
| `scrape.ts` compiles and runs with tsx without errors | ✅ Verified |
| Outputs valid JSON matching `Country[]` schema (country, code, avgTemps with all 12 MonthKey fields) | ✅ 187 entries |
| At least 150 country entries in output | ✅ 187 countries |
| All temperature values are numbers in Celsius | ✅ Validated |

Closes #354
